title: Web Docs Doc Spec — the generation-layout + options contract the generator conforms to
summary: Prep research for decision #1163 (Doc Spec slice of the #1038 webdocs spec-surface epic). The webdocs product is built (#424/#425/#427) but its WE-layer spec is greenfield — webdocs is a status:poc stub on the WE side (we:src/_data/projects/webdocs.json) with no WE code; the only running impl is FUI's generator (fui:webdocs/generator.ts, from #424). So the Doc Spec is a contract authored OVER a shipped impl, constrained by the #091 docs-as-code ruling (the customer's webmanifest+webcases are the source of truth, Plateau never owns content). The shipped generator already pins most of it implicitly: WebManifest { id, name, description?, blocks? } where blocks? is the only layout-bearing field (fui:webdocs/generator.ts:83-89); a FLAT DocsSite { pages: DocsPage[] } block-keyed data model, not rendered HTML (92-103); layout rules all inside generateDocsSite (112-118) — pages follow manifest.blocks order else alphabetic, manifest is scope source-of-truth, empty pages produced; and ZERO options (the pure (manifest, cases) → DocsSite signature). SURVEY — the six leading docs generators split into three archetypes on a derived↔authored spectrum: pure declarative manifest (Mintlify docs.json navigation.tabs[].groups[].pages[], VitePress themeConfig.sidebar), hybrid autogenerate+overrides (Docusaurus {type:autogenerated,dirName}+sidebar_position, Starlight autogenerate:{directory}+sidebar.order), fully derived from source (Storybook Meta.title A/B/C slash-path + storySort, TypeDoc nav as a projection of the type graph). Findings: hierarchy is universal at the sidebar level — flat survives only in the top nav, so the generator's flat block-keyed list is the immature shape (mature end-state is hierarchical, making flat-vs-hierarchical a now-vs-later prioritization, not a merit fork); the more derived a system the more it SPLITS scope from presentation (Storybook/TypeDoc), while declarative tools FOLD them into one ordered array; Mintlify (commercial docs-as-code leader) is the single-manifest archetype that #091 single-source-of-truth points at; options stay OPEN in the leaders that scale (Docusaurus sidebar_custom_props + sidebarItemsGenerator, Starlight custom attrs). PLACEMENT is settled by statute not fork — #817/#855 put the type contracts (WebManifest, WebCases, DocsSite) + a golden conformance-vector suite in WE (the @webeverything/contracts/webdocs subpath per #239/#700/#872, vectors on the we:conformance-vectors kit #899/#1016), all runtime (generateDocsSite, parseWebCase, resolveWebcasesInput) in FUI. Three genuine forks, each bold-defaulted: Fork 1 CONTRACT STRENGTH = deterministic conformance-locked mapping rules + golden vectors so any generator is byte-equivalent (vs thin type-only — coherent but breaks #091 no-drift between Plateau-hosted and self-hosted output; echoes the #463 module-service determinism precedent); Fork 2 NAVIGATION MODEL = declarative-manifest authority + derived fallback, manifest is the authoritative TOC and blocks-omitted derives from cases alphabetically (vs pure-derived Storybook model — contradicts #091 manifest-is-scope-SoT); Fork 3 OPTIONS LOCUS = fold into the manifest as an open declarative docs.* extension, meta-schema standardized not the list (vs a separate imperative DocsOptions arg — re-introduces a second source that drifts from the manifest, breaking single-source). Full grounding in reports/2026-06-20-webdocs-doc-spec-generation-contract.md.
